fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Montana Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Montana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity montana, how to transfer physical therapist license to montana, physical therapist compact montana, montana physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Montana Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
board.mailing_address:
Montana Department of Labor & Industry
Business Standards Division
PO Box 200513
Helena, MT 59620-0513

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Montana.

steps.title: Create a POL Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the Montana Professional Online Licensing System (POL) at https://ebiz.mt.gov/POL/Default.aspx.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Log into your POL account, initiate a new application for a Physical Therapist license (selecting 'Endorsement' if applicable), complete all required sections, and pay the application fee through the system.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your professional physical therapy education program(s) to be sent directly from the institution to the Montana Board of Physical Therapy Examiners.

steps.title: Verify Licensure and NPTE Scores
steps.description: Request official verification of all current and past PT licenses from other states/jurisdictions, ensuring they are sent directly to the Montana Board. If required, have your NPTE scores sent directly from FSBPT.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the POL application or application packet to complete the required fingerprint background check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Montana physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $100. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Montana have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Montana participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Montana without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Montana?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 8 to 16 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Montana
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Montana, navigate to the Montana Professional Online Licensing System (POL) to create an account and submit your initial application.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Montana Professional Online Licensing System (POL) at https://ebiz.mt.gov/POL/Default.aspx. Click 'New User Registration' to create a new account, then log in and select 'Apply for a new license' to begin your Physical Therapist application.
